ops.body: Follow-up scope complete: synckit v0.11.0 (a declared WatchDirs entry may be a regular file, watched individually — commit 35035a90, CI + release green) and cookiesync v0.11.0 (declares each endpoint's Cookies DB file instead of the profile directory; synckit dep v0.8.0→v0.11.0 drops WatchSpec.Backend — commit a15f0200, CI + release green). Host rollout done in order (synckitd 0.11.0 → cookiesync 0.11.0 → cookiesync install → synckitd install restored the booted-out serve agent). Result: synckitd fd count collapsed ~63k → 415 total (129 DIR watches for reposync VCS leaves, 2 Cookies-file watches for arc:Default + chrome:Profile 3, rest process baseline). Live-converge-from-browser-write is unobservable on this host right now: the watch engine records in memory and logs nothing on fire, and the only peer (yasyf-home) is down so the breaker suppresses the notify path silently — organic confirmation will appear as "watch: peer recovered" + notify traffic when the peer returns. Every observable layer is verified: file watches established in the serve log, kqueue file-watch semantics covered by synckit's -race tests on darwin+linux, cookiesync's watchItem path pinned by a new engine test, and codex xhigh review confirmed event-visibility correctness under rollback-journal SQLite.
